flashcards.back: A simple machine that can be in one of a finite number of states and can read input from a tape and move to a new state based on the current state and the input symbol
A string of characters that describes a set of strings and is used to specify a pattern in a string

flashcards.front: What is the difference between a DFA and an NFA?
flashcards.back: A DFA can only be in one state at a time, while an NFA can be in multiple states at the same time

flashcards.front: What is the purpose of the Kleene closure in regular expressions?
flashcards.back: To match any string that is the concatenation of zero or more strings, each matching the regular expression

flashcards.front: How do you convert a regular expression to a finite automaton?
flashcards.back: By parsing the regular expression into a syntax tree and then converting the syntax tree into a finite automaton
